f29c51491b44.instruction: Use COUNT(*) in a SELECT query to count rows in a table, e.g., SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Playlist

274e84d7c110.instruction: Use LEFT JOIN on PlaylistTrack without grouping by PlaylistId, causing duplicate counts: cursor.execute('SELECT Playlist.Name, COUNT(Track.TrackId) FROM Playlist LEFT JOIN PlaylistTrack ON Playlist.PlaylistId = PlaylistTrack.PlaylistId LEFT JOIN Track ON PlaylistTrack.TrackId = Track.TrackId')
Use CROSS JOIN between Playlist and Track, producing Cartesian product: cursor.execute('SELECT Playlist.Name, COUNT(Track.TrackId) FROM Playlist CROSS JOIN Track')

Use INNER JOIN on PlaylistTrack but omit the join to Track, counting only PlaylistTrack rows: cursor.execute('SELECT Playlist.Name, COUNT(PlaylistTrack.TrackId) FROM Playlist INNER JOIN PlaylistTrack ON Playlist.PlaylistId = PlaylistTrack.PlaylistId')
